sectionOrderRules: Hero must include sjötransport som verksamhet, relevant geografisk kontext if available and a primary CTA.
Service-summary must describe the main transport offering concretely, such as gods, passagerare, charter or specialisttransport, without vague claims.
Routes-overview should appear before the final CTA when destination or traffic-area information exists.
Trust-proof must stay specific to visible facts such as organisation details, references or practical experience, and must not invent certifications or fleet size.
Sections should avoid repeating the same promise in hero, trust-proof and final CTA.
Services-intro must connect the company identity to actual sjötransport services.
Service-list items must be concrete and describe what is transported, for whom or under what operating setup.
If pricing is shown, it should present starting models, examples or quote-based information honestly rather than fabricated fixed rates.
Routes-intro must explain whether the company serves fixed routes, destination areas, charter assignments or tailored transport solutions.
Coverage-list must use real place names or real operating areas when supplied, not generic nationwide claims unless supported.
Map may complement but not replace readable text about destination coverage.
About-story must be specific about the company and avoid generic quality slogans.
Operations-overview should explain how assignments are handled, such as planning, loading, coordination or customer communication, when such data exists.
Team is optional to render if no identifiable people are supplied, but the section slot remains part of the route grammar.
Phone, email and address must all be present and must not be placeholders.
Contact-form-cta should invite transport or booking enquiries in plain language.
Opening-hours should only be shown when actual staffed times are known.
